Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.

response_raw:
{
  "mode": "human",
  "topic": "How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.",
  "opinion": "As a 45-year-old immigrant woman living in Chicago, working as an electrician and studying while raising two kids on my own, I see fake content on social media as a real headache, especially since I only check it a few times a month. With my high openness, I'm curious about new things, but my low conscientiousness means I don't always dig deep into details, and my high neuroticism makes me worry easily about misinformation affecting my family or community. Being Muslim, I value truth and honesty, so fake posts that spread lies or hate really bother me. To manage this, I rely on my practical skills from my job—like checking sources just as I'd inspect wiring for safety. I don't have formal education, but I learned from my parents, who had different educational backgrounds, to question things and seek reliable info. When I see something suspicious, I pause and think before sharing, maybe asking friends or looking it up on trusted sites. Volunteering has taught me to help others, so I gently correct fake news when I can, without getting into arguments, since my low extraversion and agreeableness mean I prefer quiet, direct actions. Hiking keeps me grounded, reminding me that real life matters more than online drama. Overall, I focus on protecting my kids and staying informed through simple, cautious steps, avoiding social media traps by limiting my time and trusting my gut.",

Opinion integrates these to propose cautious, practical strategies for handling fake content.",

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion: As a 45-year-old immigrant woman living in Chicago, working as an electrician and studying while raising two kids on my own, I see fake content on social media as a real headache, especially since I only check it a few times a month. With my high openness, I'm curious about new things, but my low conscientiousness means I don't always dig deep into details, and my high neuroticism makes me worry easily about misinformation affecting my family or community. Being Muslim, I value truth and honesty, so fake posts that spread lies or hate really bother me. To manage this, I rely on my practical skills from my job—like checking sources just as I'd inspect wiring for safety. I don't have formal education, but I learned from my parents, who had different educational backgrounds, to question things and seek reliable info. When I see something suspicious, I pause and think before sharing, maybe asking friends or looking it up on trusted sites. Volunteering has taught me to help others, so I gently correct fake news when I can, without getting into arguments, since my low extraversion and agreeableness mean I prefer quiet, direct actions. Hiking keeps me grounded, reminding me that real life matters more than online drama. Overall, I focus on protecting my kids and staying informed through simple, cautious steps, avoiding social media traps by limiting my time and trusting my gut.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: Derived opinion from persona traits: age 45 suggests life experience; occupation as electrician implies practicality; immigrant status may relate to caution with misinformation; no formal education but parents' education influences critical thinking; high openness leads to curiosity but low conscientiousness reduces thorough verification; high neuroticism increases concern about fake content; low extraversion and agreeableness favor independent, non-confrontational management; religious beliefs emphasize truth; limited social media use shapes a hands-off approach; hobbies like volunteering and hiking inform community-minded and grounded perspectives. Opinion integrates these to propose cautious, practical strategies for handling fake content.
response_parsed.tone: Practical, concerned, grounded, and slightly cautious, with a focus on personal responsibility and community values.
